articles.title: Deloitte India aims to become country's biggest professional services firm by 2028
articles.description: Deloitte India is on track to claim the title of the nation's premier professional services provider by 2028, fueled by an expansion strategy that includes doubling its workforce to 100,000 and aiming for $5 billion in revenue. Through Project Bharat, the firm is not only targeting larger corporations but also reaching out to small businesses and innovative startups.

articles.title: Are More Hikes In Fuel Prices Coming? Keep Following Timesnownews.com For All The Latest Updates As Fuel Prices Climb Up
articles.description: India saw its first fuel price increase in over four years, with petrol and diesel rising by Rs 3 per litre due to elevated global crude oil prices and supply disruptions from the ongoing Iran war. But is the latest price hike is the final one or just the first installment.

articles.title: ET Graphics: BoP stability tested by outflows & Rupee fall
articles.description: India's external accounts are under strain. The current account deficit is widening, and capital may flow out. The rupee has weakened significantly. Policymakers are considering various measures beyond interest rate hikes to stabilize the currency. These include incentives for foreign deposits and liberalizing capital inflows. State-owned banks may also issue foreign currency bonds.

articles.title: Strong digital business boosts ENIL FY26 revenue 3.9% to Rs 565 crore
articles.description: Entertainment Network India, or ENIL, saw its revenue climb to ₹565 crore in FY26. This growth was powered by its digital business, which expanded significantly. The company's radio advertising market faced challenges. ENIL's digital segment now contributes substantially to its overall revenue. The company maintained a strong financial position and recommended a dividend.

articles.title: Konkan Railway undertakes preventive measures and thorough pre-monsoon inspection of its network
articles.description: Konkan Railway Corporation Limited (KRCL), in preparation for the upcoming monsoon, has been undertaking preventive measures and thorough pre-monsoon track inspections to ensure the safety and reliability of train operations during the rainy season.

articles.title: Over 5,000 metro piers and portals to get advertisements across Bengaluru

articles.title: Govt. will invest ₹1 cr. for raising grafted cashew saplings: Eshwar Khandre
articles.description: Minister for Forest, Environment and Ecology Eshwar Khandre said here on Sunday that the government will invest ₹1 crore for raising and distributing grafted cashew saplings for farmers through the forest department and Karnataka Cashew Development Corporation (KCDC).

Maharashtra govt to roll out farm loan waiver before June 30: Devendra Fadnavis
articles.description: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis confirmed a farm loan waiver will be implemented before June 30. Preparations are in the final stages. He also addressed the Maratha reservation issue, stating the government has consistently supported the community. Activist Manoj Jarange's planned agitation was noted. The Chief Minister clarified the process for issuing Kunbi caste certificates adheres to Supreme Court directives.

articles.description: The settlement terms agreed to by Snap, YouTube, and TikTok-owner ByteDance were not disclosed. Meta, by contrast, is set to head to court in June to fight the case.

articles.title: Balrampur Chini aims ₹ 2,000 cr revenue from new bio-plastic plant in UP
articles.description: Sugar firm Balrampur Chini Mills Ltd expects about Rs 2,000 crore annual revenue from its new bio-plastic plant in Uttar Pradesh, scheduled to be commissioned during the December quarter, a senior company official said. Balrampur Chini Mills, which has 10 sugar factories in Uttar Pradesh, is setting up a Poly Lactic Acid (PLA) plant of 80,000 tonnes per annum capacity at a cost of more than Rs 3,000 crore. In an interview with PTI, Balrampur Chini Executive Director Avantika Saraogi said, "This new plant will be operational in the third quarter of this fiscal." Asked about the revenue potential from the plant, she said, "We are expecting a revenue of about Rs 2,000 crore at full capacity. Balrampur Chini posted a total revenue of around Rs 6,300 crore during the last fiscal. The full-year impact of this new PLA plant would be reflected in the 2027-28 fiscal. Saraogi said the construction activities on the site are undergoing at full speed. Meanwhile, the company is doing analysis

articles.title: West Bengal rice industry seeks infra push, faster clearances from BJP govt
articles.description: The rice industry in West Bengal, the country's largest rice-producing state, has sought better infrastructure, faster approvals and stronger export promotion from the new BJP government in the state to boost growth and investments in the sector. Ricevilla Foods CEO Suraj Agarwal said the industry was looking for improvement in infrastructure for rice mills and warehouses, particularly in rural areas where poor roads, drainage systems and delayed electricity connections continue to affect operations. "In many rural districts, electricity connections for new rice mills take months, causing delays, higher project costs and harassment. The industry expects faster services and proper infrastructure support from the government," Agarwal said. He said modern storage and logistics infrastructure has become increasingly important as India's annual rice production crossed 1,500 lakh tonnes and government rice stocks exceeded 590 lakh tonnes. The industry has also demanded a single-window ..

articles.title: PFC board clears next step for REC merger, seeks govt approval
articles.description: Power Finance Corporation has taken a key step toward merging with REC Limited after its board approved seeking the President of India’s nod for the transaction. PFC has authorised CMD Parminder Chopra to seek formal approval. The merger will be based on a share swap ratio determined by valuers, while ensuring the merged entity retains its government-company status.

articles.title: Karnataka revises liquor price, becomes first Indian State to have AIB-based excise duty model
articles.description: Karnataka introduces India's first AIB-based liquor tax system, cutting IML slabs from 16 to 8 to lower liquor prices and match neighbouring states.

articles.title: 8th Pay Commission Update: What the New DA Changes Could Mean for Your Salary
articles.description: 8th Pay Commission: Central government employees are making a huge demand ahead of the 8th Pay Commission. According to employee unions, if this demand is accepted, it will directly impact the salary, allowances, and pension of lakhs of employees.

articles.title: CBSE OSM controversy: Board cuts revaluation, answer sheet verification charge
articles.description: CBSE has reduced revaluation and answer sheet verification charges to Rs 100, addressing student anxiety over On-Screen Marking. The board emphasized OSM's benefits of uniform evaluation and adherence to marking schemes. Transparent avenues for result review, including script access and re-evaluation, are available to students.

articles.title: 30% Indians face loan rejection without knowing why. Can AI solve this credit awareness gap?
articles.description: Nearly 30% of Indians have faced loan or credit card rejection without understanding the reason, highlighting a major gap in credit awareness. Industry experts now believe AI-powered tools could help decode credit profiles and provide borrowers with personalized guidance before they apply.

articles.title: Indian banks' asset quality stable despite Gulf war worries: Report
articles.description: Indian banks maintained stable asset quality in the March quarter despite Gulf war concerns. Brokerage firm Systematix warns the real impact may come later but holds a positive outlook on the sector, citing support from government schemes.

articles.title: Meta employees reportedly stuffed bags with snacks before layoffs hit - ‘felt like doomsday’
articles.description: A former Meta employee shared a striking detail about the night before the company's first major layoff round, describing it as "doomsday" with employees packing free office snacks, chargers, and drinks. This anecdote has resonated widely online, highlighting the pervasive anxiety and emotional toll of job insecurity within Big Tech.

articles.title: Is petrol cheaper in Bhutan than India? Here’s the truth
articles.description: A viral social media video falsely claims petrol is cheaper in Bhutan than in India. The Press Information Bureau (PIB) has debunked this, stating the video is old and shared out of context to spread misinformation. Recent fuel price hikes in India and comparisons with neighboring countries highlight the misleading nature of the claim.

articles.title: Holding stocks is harder than finding them: 10 mid- and small-caps stocks that may be worth holding on to
articles.description: Holding a stock is not an act of blind faith. It is not refusing to change one’s mind. It is not averaging down because the stock has fallen. Holding is an active decision. It means asking whether the reason you bought the stock is still valid. Has the price fallen because the market is nervous, or because the company’s economics have weakened? Has the macro shock permanently damaged the business, or only delayed the earnings cycle? Is the company still sitting in a sector where demand, regulation, market structure, or execution capability gives it an edge?

articles.title: Mohali consumer panel orders developer to hand over plot after 15-year delay or refund buyer with interest
articles.description: The commission held Bajwa Developers Limited deficient in service for failing to execute the sale deed despite receiving the full payment from the buyer years ago.

articles.title: Flying from Japan? Your air ticket may soon cost more
articles.description: International travel from Japan will become costlier this summer. Japan Airlines and All Nippon Airways are raising fuel surcharges on international tickets. This change applies to bookings made in May and June 2026. Higher prices are linked to increased jet fuel costs. Flights to North America and Europe will see significant hikes. This move reflects global oil supply disruptions.
